tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: And, as a lamb, having been led,

syn.mc: Participial phrase continuing the sequence begun in line 50.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; ὡς marks the aspect under which the subject is presented.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Participle: ἀχθείς. Predicative complement under ὡς: ἀμνός.
syn.ptc: ἀχθείς is circumstantial, parallel to τεχθείς in line 50.
syn.rhet: Second member of the participial crescendo: Son -> lamb.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: And, as a sheep, having been slaughtered,

syn.mc: Participial phrase continuing the same chain.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Participle: σφαγείς. Predicative complement under ὡς: πρόβατον.
syn.ptc: σφαγείς is circumstantial, extending the sacrificial sequence.
syn.rhet: Third member of the participial crescendo: lamb -> sheep.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: And, as a man, having been buried,

syn.mc: Participial phrase concluding the preparatory sequence.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Participle: ταφείς. Predicative complement under ὡς: ἄνθρωπος.
syn.ptc: ταφείς is circumstantial and leads directly into the finite verb of line 55.
syn.rhet: Fourth member of the participial crescendo: Son, lamb, sheep, man.

src: ἀνέστη ἐκ νεκρῶν ὧς θεὸς φύσει θεὸς ὧν καὶ ἄνθρωπος.
ana: ἀνέστη ἐκ νεκρῶν ὡς θεὸς φύσει θεὸς ὢν καὶ ἄνθρωπος.
tok: pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: He rose from the dead as God, being by nature God and man.
sm: He rose from the dead as God, being by nature both God and man.
emd: Followed change_log.txt for ὧς -> ὡς. The raw ὧν after θεὸς is syntactically unintelligible here and is analyzed cautiously as likely ὢν.

syn.mc: Main clause concluding the participial sequence of lines 50-54.
syn.sub: No subordinate finite clause; the participle ὢν adds an explanatory circumstantial qualification.
syn.svo: Main clause subject: Christ. Verb: ἀνέστη. Prepositional phrase: ἐκ νεκρῶν. Circumstantial complement: ὡς θεός. Participial qualification: φύσει θεὸς ὢν καὶ ἄνθρωπος.
syn.ptc: ὢν explains the ontological basis of the resurrection statement: by nature God and man.
syn.rhet: The finite climax answers the preparatory participles: burial gives way to resurrection.

emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized as punctuation.

syn.mc: Relative clause summing up the christological sequence.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause beyond the relative linkage itself; the line introduces the following catalog of aspects.
syn.svo: Subject: ὅς. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate expression: τὰ πάντα.
syn.rhet: Brief totalizing formula that opens the eightfold καθ᾽ ὃ-anaphora.

lit: According to which he judges, law,
sm: insofar as he judges, he is law,

syn.mc: Elliptical predicative clause dependent on line 56.
syn.sub: The relative phrase καθ᾽ ὃ marks an aspectual subdivision: 'in that respect in which'.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Verb: κρίνει. Predicate nominative: νόμος.
syn.rhet: First member of the eightfold καθ᾽ ὃ-anaphora.

lit: According to which he teaches, word,
sm: insofar as he teaches, he is word,

syn.mc: Elliptical predicative clause parallel to line 57.
syn.sub: Again governed by the aspectual relative phrase καθ᾽ ὃ.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Verb: διδάσκει. Predicate nominative: λόγος.
syn.rhet: Second member of the anaphoric catalog: law / word.

lit: According to which he saves, grace,
sm: insofar as he saves, he is grace,

syn.sub: The καθ᾽ ὃ phrase continues the distributive pattern.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Verb: σώζει. Predicate nominative: χάρις.
syn.rhet: Third member of the catalog, moving from juridical and didactic roles to salvific grace.

lit: According to which he begets, father,
sm: insofar as he begets, he is father,

syn.sub: The aspectual relative phrase is repeated without variation.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Verb: γεννᾷ. Predicate nominative: πατήρ.
syn.rhet: Fourth member, shifting to generative language.

lit: According to which he is begotten, son,
sm: insofar as he is begotten, he is son,

syn.mc: Elliptical predicative clause paired with line 60.
syn.sub: The repeated καθ᾽ ὃ distributes another aspect of the same subject.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Verb: γεννᾶται. Predicate nominative: υἱός.
syn.rhet: Fifth member, balancing Father with Son.

lit: According to which he suffers, sheep,
sm: insofar as he suffers, he is a sheep,

syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Verb: πάσχει. Predicate nominative: πρόβατον.
syn.rhet: Sixth member, returning to sacrificial imagery.

lit: According to which he is buried, man,
sm: insofar as he is buried, he is man,

syn.sub: The distributive καθ᾽ ὃ formula remains in force.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Verb: θάπτεται. Predicate nominative: ἄνθρωπος.
syn.rhet: Seventh member, balancing suffering as sheep with burial as man.

lit: According to which he rises, God.
sm: insofar as he rises, he is God.

syn.mc: Elliptical predicative clause concluding the catalog.
syn.sub: Final member of the repeated καθ᾽ ὃ structure.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Verb: ἀνίσταται. Predicate nominative: θεός.
syn.rhet: Eighth and climactic member, ending the anaphoric sequence with divine resurrection.

lit: This one is Jesus the Christ,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the line resumes direct assertion after the catalog.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτος. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate nominative/appositional name: ᾿Ιησοῦς ὁ Χριστός.
syn.app: ὁ Χριστός stands in apposition to ᾿Ιησοῦς.
syn.rhet: The sprawling christological description resolves into explicit naming.

src: ᾧ ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶι αἰώνων. ἀμήν.
ana: ᾧ ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων. ἀμήν.
lit: To whom the glory unto the ages of ages. Amen.
sm: To him be glory unto the ages of ages. Amen.

syn.sub: Relative pronoun ᾧ links the doxology to Jesus Christ.
syn.svo: Implied predicate such as ἐστίν or ἔστω. Dative referent: ᾧ. Subject: ἡ δόξα. Prepositional phrase: εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων.
syn.rhet: Conventional doxological closure seals the section.

src: Τοῦτό ἐστιν τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον
lit: This is the mystery of the Passover
sm: This is the mystery of the Passover

syn.mc: Main identificatory clause opening a new section.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause in this line; explanatory comparison follows in lines 69-70.
syn.svo: Subject: Τοῦτο. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate nominative: τὸ τοῦ πάσχα μυστήριον.
syn.rhet: Resumptive formula restarts the exposition after the doxology.

lit: Just as it has been written in the law,
sm: just as it has been written in the law,

syn.mc: Subordinate comparative clause dependent on line 68.
syn.sub: καθὼς introduces the first validating comparison.
syn.svo: Verb: γέγραπται. Prepositional phrase: ἐν τῷ νόμῳ. The understood subject is the content of the mystery as attested in scripture.
syn.rhet: First scriptural-validation formula in a paired structure with line 70.

lit: As it has been read a little earlier.
sm: as it was read a little earlier.
emd: Stray OCR apostrophe/quotation removed; the line is normalized as a closed comparative clause.

syn.mc: Subordinate comparative clause coordinated with line 69.
syn.sub: ὡς introduces a second validating comparison, still dependent on line 68.
syn.svo: Verb: ἀνέγνωσται. Adverbial modifiers: μικρῷ πρόσθεν.
syn.rhet: The section grounds itself both in scriptural writing and in the immediately preceding public reading.
